compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory). Triage + decisions (all advisory; resolved by committing design decisions here):
- [E2 fbc10 path 'e.g.'] DECIDED: kernel package = src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (decide.py for Pass-3 core; __init__ re-exports).
- [E2 ffdab / E1 f741d 're-export if needed'] DECIDED: YES re-export. passes.py re-exports pass3_decide/validity/impact/severity_label/GRADED_BINARY/DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD from the kernel; orchestrator.pass3_over_findings stays as a thin registry-resolver wrapper over kernel.pass3_over_findings. Tests + workflow_ops keep their current import sites (back-compat).
- [F1 f5fa1 / E1 f0f39 / E5 fce85 test fixture + behavioral] DECIDED: offline equivalence is the existing deterministic gate-path tests (test_plan_review_workflow.test_decide_op_partitions_findings + test_plan_review pass3 behavioral cases by construction: validity 0/0.5, block/advisory/dropped/veto/indeterminate). They are ground-truth-by-construction AND pin the partition; both move/stay green pre- vs post-extraction. No new golden file needed (partition is pure-deterministic).
- [T4 f635d rollback] JUSTIFIED N/A: pure in-process refactor, no runtime flag/deploy seam; rollback = git revert. Old code is replaced in the SAME commit the equivalence test guards, so a regression cannot merge.
- [T7 f570e / T5e f39ce docs/ADR] DEFERRED to WS4 (the epic's dedicated kernel consumer-seam + doc deliverable). WS1 adds a kernel module docstring recording the pass3 contract + threshold parameterization.
- [F4 f3534 beneficiary] JUSTIFIED: the named consumer is epic b744 (code review); WS1 is structural extraction (epic scope). No value-metric AC needed for an internal refactor.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The deterministic decision math (Pass-3) is the most divergence-dangerous part of the four-pass
framework: if two gates compute "what blocks" differently, the gate guarantee silently forks.
`pass3_decide` + `pass3_over_findings` already live in `plan_review/orchestrator.py`/`passes.py`
and are *already* shared across the (former) bespoke and workflow paths — this lifts them into a
named shared kernel module as the framework's single decision core, with per-criterion thresholds
parameterized so a consuming gate can set its own posture without forking the math.

## What

- Create the kernel package (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/`) and move the deterministic
  Pass-3 core into it: `pass3_decide` (validity = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers;
  impact = mean of ordinal-mapped severity attributes; priority = validity×impact; the
  conditional cited-reference veto; block/advisory against per-criterion thresholds) +
  `pass3_over_findings`.
- Parameterize the per-criterion `block_threshold` / posture so consumers pass their own
  thresholds; keep plan-review's current defaults intact.
- Rewire the plan-review workflow's `plan_review_decide` op to call the kernel (no behavior
  change). Keep a thin re-export from `plan_review` if needed for back-compat.

## Scope

In scope: the Pass-3 decision function + math + the binary→validity / attrs→impact maps, moved to
the kernel; plan-review consumes it. Out of scope: Pass-2 verify (WS2), the coach (WS3), the seam
doc + evals (WS4), and threshold *calibration* (deferred). Touches
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py`/`passes.py`, the new kernel module, and
`workflow_ops.py` (the decide op).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A kernel module exists and houses `pass3_decide` + `pass3_over_findings` + the
      validity/impact/veto math; per-criterion thresholds are a parameter, not hardcoded.
- [ ] The plan-review `plan_review_decide` op consumes the kernel; an offline test asserts the
      decided partition (blocking/advisory/dropped/indeterminate) is unchanged vs before.
- [ ] No second copy of the decision math remains; any `plan_review` references are thin
      re-exports, not duplicated logic.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS1: Extract Pass-3 deterministic decision into the shared review kernel
